content.problemStatement:
The lowest common divisor (LCD) of a sequence of kkk integers (x1,x2,…,xk)(x_1, x_2, \ldots, x_k)(x1​,x2​,…,xk​) is the **smallest** integer ddd such that:


d>1d\gt 1d>1, and
ddd is a **divisor** of xix_ixi​ for all 1≤i≤k1\le i \le k1≤i≤k.
If no such integer ddd exists, the LCD of the sequence is defined to be 111.

For example,


The LCD of (6,15,6,21)(6, 15, 6, 21)(6,15,6,21) is 333.
The LCD of (9,9)(9, 9)(9,9) is 333.
The LCD of (10,20)(10, 20)(10,20) is 222.
The LCD of (6,10,15)(6, 10, 15)(6,10,15) is 111.
You're given an array AAA containing NNN integers, and an integer KKK.

Count the number of non-empty subsequences of AAA whose LCD equals KKK.

Since this number can be large, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of the array and the required LCD.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of non-empty subsequences of AAA whose LCD is KKK, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
